log.before:
# OnlineCourse Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification (For Backend Developer)

| Route Path | Function Name | HTTP Method | Template File | Context Variables |
|------------|---------------|-------------|---------------|------------------|
| / | root_redirect | GET | None (redirect) | None |
| /dashboard | dashboard | GET | dashboard.html | username (str), fullname (str), enrolled_courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), progress (int)}) |
| /catalog | catalog | GET | course_catalog.html | courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), description (str), category (str), level (str), duration (str), status (str)}) |
| /catalog/course/<course_id> | course_details | GET, POST | course_details.html | course (dict: {course_id (str), title (str), description (str), category (str), level (str), duration (str), status (str)}), enrolled (bool) |
| /my-courses | my_courses | GET | my_courses.html | enrolled_courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), progress (int)}) |
| /my-courses/<course_id> | course_learning | GET, POST | course_learning.html | course (dict: {course_id (str), title (str), description (str), category (str), level (str), duration (str), status (str)}), lessons (list of dict: {lesson_id (str), title (str), content (str)}), current_lesson (dict: {lesson_id (str), title (str), content (str)}), progress (int), can_mark_complete (bool) |
| /assignments | assignments | GET | my_assignments.html | assignments (list of dict: {assignment_id (str), course_id (str), title (str), description (str), due_date (str), max_points (int), status (str: Submitted or Pending)}) |
| /assignments/submit/<assignment_id> | submit_assignment | GET, POST | submit_assignment.html | assignment (dict: {assignment_id (str), title (str), description (str)}), submission_status (str: None or Submitted or Late) |
| /certificates | certificates | GET | certificates.html | certificates (list of dict: {certificate_id (str), course_title (str), issue_date (str)}) |
| /profile | user_profile | GET, POST | profile.html | username (str), email (str), fullname (str) |

**Notes:**
- Root route '/' redirects to '/dashboard'.
- POST on /catalog/course/<course_id> handles enrollment if not already enrolled, updates enrollments.txt.
- POST on /my-courses/<course_id> handles marking current lesson complete, updates progress, and generates certificate in certificates.txt if progress reaches 100%.
- POST on /assignments/submit/<assignment_id> handles assignment submission, updates submissions.txt.
- POST on /profile updates the user profile in users.txt.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications (For Frontend Developer)

### 1. templates/dashboard.html
- Page Title: Learning Dashboard (for <title> and <h1>)
- Element IDs:
  - dashboard-page: div container
  - welcome-message: h1 element showing "Welcome, {{ fullname }}"
  - enrolled-courses: div showing list of enrolled courses with titles and progress %
  - browse-courses-button: button to navigate to course catalog
  - my-courses-button: button to navigate to my courses
- Context Variables:
  - username (str)
  - fullname (str)
  - enrolled_courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), progress (int)})
- Navigation:
  - browse-courses-button -> url_for('catalog')
  - my-courses-button -> url_for('my_courses')

---

### 2. templates/course_catalog.html
- Page Title: Available Courses
- Element IDs:
  - catalog-page: div container
  - search-input: input for searching courses
  - course-grid: div containing course cards
  - view-course-button-{{ course.course_id }}: button to view course details
  - back-to-dashboard: button to navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - courses (list of dict as defined above)
- Navigation:
  - view-course-button-{course_id} -> url_for('course_details', course_id=course.course_id)
  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')

---

### 3. templates/course_details.html
- Page Title: Course Details
- Element IDs:
  - course-details-page: div container
  - course-title: h1 displaying course.title
  - course-description: div displaying course.description
  - enroll-button: button to enroll or disabled with "Already Enrolled"
  - back-to-catalog: button to navigate back to course catalog
- Context Variables:
  - course (dict)
  - enrolled (bool)
- Navigation:
  - enroll-button (POST form submit to current route)
  - back-to-catalog -> url_for('catalog')
- Button State:
  - enroll-button disabled and text "Already Enrolled" if enrolled == True

---

### 4. templates/my_courses.html
- Page Title: My Courses
- Element IDs:
  - my-courses-page: div container
  - courses-list: div containing list of enrolled courses
  - continue-learning-button-{{ course.course_id }}: button to continue learning course
  - back-to-dashboard: button to navigate to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - enrolled_courses (list of dict)
- Navigation:
  - continue-learning-button-{course_id} -> url_for('course_learning', course_id=course.course_id)
  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')

---

### 5. templates/course_learning.html
- Page Title: Course Learning
- Element IDs:
  - learning-page: div container
  - lessons-list: div listing all lessons titles
  - lesson-content: div showing current lesson content
  - mark-complete-button: button to mark lesson complete
  - back-to-my-courses: button to go back to my courses
- Context Variables:
  - course (dict)
  - lessons (list of dict {lesson_id, title, content})
  - current_lesson (dict)
  - progress (int)
  - can_mark_complete (bool)
- Navigation:
  - mark-complete-button (POST form submit to current route)
  - back-to-my-courses -> url_for('my_courses')

---

### 6. templates/my_assignments.html
- Page Title: My Assignments
- Element IDs:
  - assignments-page: div container
  - assignments-table: table showing all assignments
  - submit-assignment-button-{{ assignment.assignment_id }}: button to submit assignment
  - back-to-dashboard: button to go back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - assignments (list of dict with status 5Submitted6 or 5Pending6)
- Navigation:
  - submit-assignment-button-{assignment_id} -> url_for('submit_assignment', assignment_id=assignment.assignment_id)
  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')

---

### 7. templates/submit_assignment.html
- Page Title: Submit Assignment
- Element IDs:
  - submit-page: div container
  - assignment-info: div showing assignment title and description
  - submission-text: textarea for input
  - submit-button: button to submit assignment
  - back-to-assignments: button to go back to assignments
- Context Variables:
  - assignment (dict: assignment_id, title, description)
  - submission_status (str: None, "Submitted", or "Late")
- Navigation:
  - submit-button (POST form submit to current route)
  - back-to-assignments -> url_for('assignments')

---

### 8. templates/certificates.html
- Page Title: My Certificates
- Element IDs:
  - certificates-page: div container
  - certificates-grid: div showing certificate cards
  - back-to-dashboard: button to go back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - certificates (list of dict: certificate_id, course_title, issue_date)
- Navigation:
  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')

---

### 9. templates/profile.html
- Page Title: My Profile
- Element IDs:
  - profile-page: div container
  - profile-email: input for email
  - profile-fullname: input for fullname
  - update-profile-button: button to save changes
  - back-to-dashboard: button to go back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - username (str)
  - email (str)
  - fullname (str)
- Navigation:
  - update-profile-button (POST form submit to current route)
  - back-to-dashboard -> url_for('dashboard')

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas (For Backend Developer)

### 1. data/users.txt
- Fields: username|email|fullname
- Description:
  - username: unique user identifier (str)
  - email: user email address (str)
  - fullname: user full display name (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  john|john@student.com|John Student
  alice|alice@instructor.com|Alice Professor
  jane|jane@student.com|Jane Learner
  ```

---

### 2. data/courses.txt
- Fields: course_id|title|description|category|level|duration|status
- Description:
  - course_id: unique course identifier (str)
  - title: course title (str)
  - description: full course description (str)
  - category: course category like Programming, Web, Data (str)
  - level: difficulty level (Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) (str)
  - duration: textual duration (e.g., "40 hours") (str)
  - status: course availability status (e.g., Active) (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|Python Programming|Learn Python from scratch|Programming|Beginner|40 hours|Active
  2|Web Development|Build modern websites|Web|Intermediate|60 hours|Active
  3|Data Science|Introduction to data analysis|Data|Advanced|80 hours|Active
  ```

---

### 3. data/enrollments.txt
- Fields: enrollment_id|username|course_id|enrollment_date|progress|status
- Description:
  - enrollment_id: unique enrollment identifier (str)
  - username: user who enrolled (str)
  - course_id: id of enrolled course (str)
  - enrollment_date: date string in YYYY-MM-DD (str)
  - progress: integer 0-100 (percentage completed) (int)
  - status: enrollment status ("In Progress", "Completed") (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|john|1|2024-11-01|75|In Progress
  2|jane|1|2024-10-15|100|Completed
  3|john|2|2024-11-10|25|In Progress
  ```

---

### 4. data/assignments.txt
- Fields: assignment_id|course_id|title|description|due_date|max_points
- Description:
  - assignment_id: unique assignment identifier (str)
  - course_id: course id this assignment belongs to (str)
  - title: assignment title (str)
  - description: full description (str)
  - due_date: due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (str)
  - max_points: maximum points achievable (int)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|1|Python Basics Quiz|Complete the quiz on variables and data types|2024-12-01|100
  2|1|Final Project|Build a calculator application|2024-12-15|200
  ```

---

### 5. data/submissions.txt
- Fields: submission_id|assignment_id|username|submission_text|submit_date|grade|feedback
- Description:
  - submission_id: unique submission id (str)
  - assignment_id: linked assignment id (str)
  - username: user submitting (str)
  - submission_text: text response (str)
  - submit_date: date of submission YYYY-MM-DD (str)
  - grade: numeric score or empty if not graded (str)
  - feedback: text feedback or empty if not graded (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|1|john|My answers are...|2024-11-25|85|Good work!
  2|2|jane|Here is my project...|2024-11-20|95|Excellent!
  ```

---

### 6. data/certificates.txt
- Fields: certificate_id|username|course_id|issue_date
- Description:
  - certificate_id: unique certificate id (str)
  - username: user who earned certificate (str)
  - course_id: course for which certificate issued (str)
  - issue_date: date issued YYYY-MM-DD (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|jane|1|2024-11-22
  ```

---

# OnlineCourse Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification (For Backend Developer)

| Route Path | Function Name | HTTP Method | Template File | Context Variables |
|------------|---------------|-------------|---------------|------------------|
| / | root_redirect | GET | None (redirect) | None |
| /dashboard | dashboard | GET | dashboard.html | username (str), fullname (str), enrolled_courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), progress (int)}) |
| /catalog | catalog | GET | course_catalog.html | courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), description (str), category (str), level (str), duration (str), status (str)}) |
| /catalog/course/<course_id> | course_details | GET, POST | course_details.html | course (dict: {course_id (str), title (str), description (str), category (str), level (str), duration (str), status (str)}), enrolled (bool) |
| /my-courses | my_courses | GET | my_courses.html | enrolled_courses (list of dict: {course_id (str), title (str), progress (int)}) |
| /my-courses/<course_id> | course_learning | GET, POST | course_learning.html | course (dict: {course_id (str), title (str), description (str), category (str), level (str), duration (str), status (str)}), lessons (list of dict: {lesson_id (str), title (str), content (str)}), current_lesson (dict: {lesson_id (str), title (str), content (str)}), progress (int), can_mark_complete (bool) |
| /assignments | assignments | GET | my_assignments.html | assignments (list of dict: {assignment_id (str), course_id (str), title (str), description (str), due_date (str), max_points (int), status (str: Submitted or Pending)}) |
| /assignments/submit/<assignment_id> | submit_assignment | GET, POST | submit_assignment.html | assignment (dict: {assignment_id (str), title (str), description (str)}), submission_status (str: None or Submitted or Late) |
| /certificates | certificates | GET | certificates.html | certificates (list of dict: {certificate_id (str), course_title (str), issue_date (str)}) |
| /profile | user_profile | GET, POST | profile.html | username (str), email (str), fullname (str) |

**Notes:**
- Root route '/' redirects to '/dashboard'.
- POST on /catalog/course/<course_id> handles enrollment if not already enrolled, updates enrollments.txt.
- POST on /my-courses/<course_id> handles marking current lesson complete, updates progress, and generates certificate in certificates.txt if progress reaches 100%.
- POST on /assignments/submit/<assignment_id> handles assignment submission, updates submissions.txt.
- POST on /profile updates the user profile in users.txt.

## Section 3: Data File Schemas (For Backend Developer)

### 1. data/users.txt
- Fields: username|email|fullname
- Description:
  - username: unique user identifier (str)
  - email: user email address (str)
  - fullname: user full display name (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  john|john@student.com|John Student
  alice|alice@instructor.com|Alice Professor
  jane|jane@student.com|Jane Learner
  ```

---

### 2. data/courses.txt
- Fields: course_id|title|description|category|level|duration|status
- Description:
  - course_id: unique course identifier (str)
  - title: course title (str)
  - description: full course description (str)
  - category: course category like Programming, Web, Data (str)
  - level: difficulty level (Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) (str)
  - duration: textual duration (e.g., "40 hours") (str)
  - status: course availability status (e.g., Active) (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|Python Programming|Learn Python from scratch|Programming|Beginner|40 hours|Active
  2|Web Development|Build modern websites|Web|Intermediate|60 hours|Active
  3|Data Science|Introduction to data analysis|Data|Advanced|80 hours|Active
  ```

---

### 3. data/enrollments.txt
- Fields: enrollment_id|username|course_id|enrollment_date|progress|status
- Description:
  - enrollment_id: unique enrollment identifier (str)
  - username: user who enrolled (str)
  - course_id: id of enrolled course (str)
  - enrollment_date: date string in YYYY-MM-DD (str)
  - progress: integer 0-100 (percentage completed) (int)
  - status: enrollment status ("In Progress", "Completed") (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|john|1|2024-11-01|75|In Progress
  2|jane|1|2024-10-15|100|Completed
  3|john|2|2024-11-10|25|In Progress
  ```

---

### 4. data/assignments.txt
- Fields: assignment_id|course_id|title|description|due_date|max_points
- Description:
  - assignment_id: unique assignment identifier (str)
  - course_id: course id this assignment belongs to (str)
  - title: assignment title (str)
  - description: full description (str)
  - due_date: due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (str)
  - max_points: maximum points achievable (int)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|1|Python Basics Quiz|Complete the quiz on variables and data types|2024-12-01|100
  2|1|Final Project|Build a calculator application|2024-12-15|200
  ```

---

### 5. data/submissions.txt
- Fields: submission_id|assignment_id|username|submission_text|submit_date|grade|feedback
- Description:
  - submission_id: unique submission id (str)
  - assignment_id: linked assignment id (str)
  - username: user submitting (str)
  - submission_text: text response (str)
  - submit_date: date of submission YYYY-MM-DD (str)
  - grade: numeric score or empty if not graded (str)
  - feedback: text feedback or empty if not graded (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|1|john|My answers are...|2024-11-25|85|Good work!
  2|2|jane|Here is my project...|2024-11-20|95|Excellent!
  ```

---

### 6. data/certificates.txt
- Fields: certificate_id|username|course_id|issue_date
- Description:
  - certificate_id: unique certificate id (str)
  - username: user who earned certificate (str)
  - course_id: course for which certificate issued (str)
  - issue_date: date issued YYYY-MM-DD (str)
- Examples:
  ```
  1|jane|1|2024-11-22
  ```

---
